b:T45b5,<div class="specification">
<p>The diagram shows part of two neurons.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: © International Baccalaureate Organization 2020.]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline how the amphipathic properties of phospholipids play a role in membrane structure.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the name of the structure shown.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>X indicates the movement of a structure in the neuron. Explain what events trigger this movement and what happens next.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div>c:T517,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. part hydrophobic/not attracted to water/non-polar <em><strong>AND</strong></em> part hydrophilic/attracted to water/polar; <em>Both needed</em>.</p>
<p>b. bilayer formed (formed naturally by phospholipids in water);</p>
<p>c. <span style="text-decoration: underline;">hydrophilic</span> heads/parts face outwards <em><strong>AND</strong></em> <span style="text-decoration: underline;">hydrophobic</span> tails/parts face inwards;</p>
<p><em>Do not allow water loving/hating in mpa or mpc</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>synapse/synaptic</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. depolarization of pre-synaptic membrane / action potential/nerve impulse arrives;</p>
<p>b. uptake of calcium / calcium ions diffuse in / calcium channels open;</p>
<p>c. structures containing neurotransmitter/vesicles move to/fuse with membrane;</p>
<p>d. <span style="text-decoration: underline;">neurotransmitter</span>/<span style="text-decoration: underline;">acetylcholine</span> released by exocytosis into cleft/binds to postsynaptic membrane/receptors;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/1/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","1"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"20N.2.SL.TZ0.3"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"20N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","1-3-membrane-structure, 6-5-neurons-and-synapses"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Knowledge of amphipathic properties in part a was good. In b, most could state that the diagram shows a synapse. A variety of spelling is usually allowed, but in this case ‘synapsis’ was not allowed in English as this refers to the pairing of homologous chromosomes. There were some G2 comments that the X could be taken as the structure. However X clearly points to an arrow, not a structure. Most were able to gain some marks by describing the events, but some lost marks by not clearly indicating that it is the vesicle containing the neurotransmitter that moves.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.i.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.ii.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
